<findings>

[observation] [info] High cohesion (0.8) — files are tightly interconnected
[observation] [info] Bidirectional imports with hench-core (6 in, 4 out) indicate appropriate integration pattern for a guard layer
[observation] [info] Good cohesion (0.8) shows guard modules form a coherent security boundary
[observation] [info] Having tools/files.ts, tools/git.ts, and tools/shell.ts here correctly co-locates guarded operations with their validators
[pattern] [info] Path and command validation split across multiple files enables granular testing but requires careful coordination of allow/deny list updates
[relationship] [warning] Security policy enforcement (guard) importing from protected code (core tools) creates potential for policy bypass if tool implementations change
[anti-pattern] [info] Path validation (paths.ts) and command validation (commands.ts) share no common interface despite both being validators — missing PolicyValidator abstraction
[anti-pattern] [warning] tools.ts in agent/ directory imports guard but guard/commands.ts imports types from agent — circular dependency path requires interface extraction
[suggestion] [info] paths.ts and commands.ts define parallel security policies without a common Policy interface — opportunity to unify validation patterns
[suggestion] [warning] tools.ts imports from guard/commands.ts directly instead of through guard/index.ts barrel — creates implicit coupling that bypasses the public API

</findings>

<insights>

- High cohesion (0.8) — files are tightly interconnected
- Centralizes all security policy enforcement for shell and file operations
- Clean separation allows security rules to be audited independently from agent logic
- Guard zone has outbound-only logical dependency (enforces policy on core operations) but implementation shows bidirectional imports
- Guard index.ts re-exports create coupling point — consumers import guard utilities through single barrel file
- Guard barrel file (guard/index.ts) re-exports commands.ts and paths.ts but tools.ts imports directly from guard/commands — inconsistent import patterns
- paths.ts and commands.ts have parallel structure (both are policy definition files) but no shared base type or validation interface

</insights>
